ddls:
CREATE TABLE "participants" (
  "participant_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the participant */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the participant */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the participant */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest level of education completed by the participant */,
  "language_proficiency" TEXT /* Language proficiency of the participant */,
  "participation_date" TEXT /* Date when the participant took part in the experiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "images" (
  "image_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each image */,
  "image_path" TEXT /* Path to the image file */,
  "image_description" TEXT /* Description of the image */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("image_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "words" (
  "word_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each word */,
  "word" TEXT /* The word stimulus */,
  "word_type" TEXT /* Type of the word */,
  "word_frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the word in the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("word_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "experiments" (
  "experiment_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each experiment */,
  "participant_id" TEXT /* Foreign key linking to the participant table */,
  "image_id" TEXT /* Foreign key linking to the image table */,
  "word_id" TEXT /* Foreign key linking to the word table */,
  "practice" INTEGER /* Whether the trial was part of the practice session */,
  "block" INTEGER /* Block number of the trial */,
  "trial_order" INTEGER /* Order of the trial within the block */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "experiment_id" TEXT /* Foreign key linking to the experiment table */,
  "participant_id" TEXT /* Foreign key linking to the participant table */,
  "word_id" TEXT /* Foreign key linking to the word table */,
  "image_id" TEXT /* Foreign key linking to the image table */,
  "response" TEXT /* Participant's response */,
  "typing_rt" REAL /* Typing response time (in milliseconds) */,
  "type_time" REAL /* Time taken to type the response (in milliseconds) */,
  "rt" REAL /* Total response time (in milliseconds) */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response */,
  "response_accuracy" INTEGER /* Whether the response was correct or not */,
  "response_comments" TEXT /* Any comments or notes about the response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id")
)
